scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculated with higher of long or short positions included per sub-margin group
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin is correctly calculated by including only positions from the side with higher absolute market value for each sub-margin group and applying the margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant has positions across multiple sub-margin groups defined on HKEX website
Sub-group 1: Long positions total absolute market value 30,000,000 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 60,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 2: Long positions total absolute market value 750,000 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 0 HKD
Sub-group 3: Long positions total absolute market value 0 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 300,000 HKD
Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are 12% for sub-group 1, 30% for sub-group 2, 55% for sub-group 3
A flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned per DWH0081C report
when: The flat rate margin calculation is performed following Step 1 (compare long vs short absolute market values per sub-margin group), Step 2 (sum product of included positions' absolute market value and flat margin rate), and Step 3 (apply margin multiplier)
then: Sub-group 1: Short positions included (60,000,000 > 30,000,000), long positions excluded
Sub-group 2: Long positions included (750,000 > 0), short positions excluded
Sub-group 3: Short positions included (300,000 > 0), long positions excluded
Flat rate margin before multiplier = 60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55% = 7,590,000 HKD
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = 7,590,000 × 2 = 15,180,000 HKD

evidence.quote: take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them
all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation while all long sub-group 1 positions will be excluded
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin when sub-margin group has positions only on one side
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin correctly handles sub-margin groups where positions exist only on one side (all long or all short), with zero value on the opposite side
given: A clearing participant has positions in a sub-margin group
The sub-margin group has only long positions with total absolute market value of 750,000 HKD
The sub-margin group has zero short positions (total absolute market value of 0 HKD)
Flat margin rate under FieldType 3 is 30% for this sub-margin group
when: The flat rate margin calculation compares long and short absolute market values for the sub-margin group
then: Long positions are included in flat rate margin calculation (750,000 > 0)
Short positions are excluded since their total absolute market value is zero
Flat rate margin contribution from this sub-margin group = 750,000 × 30% = 225,000 HKD

evidence.quote: all long sub-group 2 positions... will be included in the flat rate margin calculation
take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them
